title: night: RSVP source tracking + IG-ratio flyer crops
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Two small additions to the /night event page setup:

- Hidden `came_from` field on the RSVP form, filled from a `?src=` URL parameter (and remembered in sessionStorage). Scheduled social posts link with `?src=li` / `?src=fb` / `?src=x`, so each RSVP email shows which channel drove it. UI-only, no backend.
- `night-flyer-4x5.jpg` and `night-flyer-square.jpg`: the flyer padded on its own cream ground to Instagram-compatible ratios, referenced by scheduled Buffer posts.

Already deployed to production manually; this PR keeps the next deploy from overwriting it.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Follow the RSVP pollen
gist.blurb: Night RSVPs now carry a tiny trail of pollen from the post that sent them. The flyer also has proper Instagram-shaped homes, because rectangles remain surprisingly political.
gist.summary: The /night RSVP form now records a ?src= value such as li, fb, or x in a hidden came_from field and remembers it with sessionStorage, while dedicated 4:5 and square cream-padded flyer crops support scheduled Instagram posts.
gist.impact: Event organizers can see which social channel brought each RSVP email, and social posts now use flyer images sized cleanly for Instagram feeds.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Polly as a cozy event promoter tracing little pollen trails from three tiny social-post signposts into an RSVP envelope, beside a cream-framed square and 4:5 event flyer display. The visual metaphor is attribution without surveillance: friendly labeled paths, a clipboard, and blooming flowers around a warm night-event noticeboard.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body and dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings, wearing a tiny event-promoter beret and holding a clipboard beside a warm cream RSVP noticeboard. Little pollen trails lead from three tiny social-post signs labeled “LI”, “FB”, and “X” into an RSVP envelope, with square and 4:5 cream-padded flyer frames, flowers, and code-plants nearby; cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, prominent lime green (#ecf874), warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.
